comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: AC ADR-number correction: the AC named ADR 0020, but 0020 was taken by the two-vote-CI-gate ADR (epic 1fa8) that landed first, so the completion-aware ADR is filed as docs/adr/0024-completion-aware-container-plan-review.md (content complete; cross-refs added to ADR 0008/0002). Updated the AC's 0020 references to 0024 to match the delivered artifact.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

A re-fired container review re-litigates delivered AC (false BLOCKs / stale coaching). We need a
deterministic gate that drops those findings without hiding anything live — mirroring the novelty
floor (ADR 0008) and drift-refresh (ADR 0002).

## What

Add a third Pass-3 floor in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` (`pass3_decide`) that, using the
completion sub-answers (story: Pass-2 sub-call) and the delivered-now predicate (story:
delivered-now), DROPS a finding iff ALL hold: attribution = a delivered-now child AND
containment = limited-to-closed AND layer = plan-semantics AND priority (validity x impact) < floor
AND the criterion is NOT in the always-preserve set (config-backed `verify.completion_preserve_criteria`; default the registered security overlay T5c + contract criterion T10). Recompute every run (never persist a drop). Record each
drop to the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (`sidecar.py`) joinable by finding id. Gate behind a
`verify.completion_floor_active` evidence-gate flag + a `verify.completion_priority_floor` in
`src/rebar/config.py` (VerifyConfig), defaulting inert — the total back-out, exactly like the
novelty flags.

## Scope

- IN: the Pass-3 drop predicate; the config keys; sidecar `floored` recording; the deterministic
  aggregation of the three sub-answers + priority + structural criterion class.
- OUT: the sub-questions themselves (separate story); the delivered-now predicate (separate story).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pass3_decide` drops iff attribution=delivered-now AND containment=limited-to-closed AND layer=plan-semantics AND priority<floor AND the finding's criterion is NOT in the always-preserve set; all other combinations keep.
- [ ] Suppression is inert unless `verify.completion_floor_active` is true; `verify.completion_priority_floor` is configurable (mirrors `novelty_priority_floor`).
- [ ] Every drop is written to the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (joinable by finding id) and is recomputed each run (no persisted suppression).
- [ ] The always-preserve set is CONFIG-BACKED (`verify.completion_preserve_criteria`) referencing REGISTERED criterion IDs — default {T5c security overlay, T10 endpoint/interface contract}; adding privacy/compliance is a config change, not code (T8/T5e).
- [ ] Completion drops carry a distinct `drop_reason="completion"` in the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (vs novelty `"novelty"` / normal), so `plan_review_result_v1` offline joins never collide (G6).
- [ ] A finding on a preserve-set (T5c/T10) criterion is never dropped regardless of the other axes.
- [ ] Unit tests cover the truth table (each single-condition flip preserves the finding).

## Worked example (suppression boundary)

Epic mid-flight; child X = closed+attested, child Y = open.
- "X's AC wording for step 3 is ambiguous" -> attribution=X(delivered-now), containment=limited-to-closed, layer=plan-semantics, priority<floor, criterion=COH -> DROPPED (settled plan text).
- "X's step-3 mechanism conflicts with Y's planned approach" -> containment=spans-open -> KEPT.
- "X's delivered contract omits an error field consumers rely on" -> layer=delivered-functionality -> KEPT.
- "X's endpoint has no auth" (T5c) -> criterion in {security} veto -> KEPT regardless of the other axes.
- If X was force-closed (not attested) -> delivered-now=False -> every finding on X KEPT.

## Acceptance Criteria (durable record + invariants)

- [ ] A durable ADR `docs/adr/0024-completion-aware-container-plan-review.md` codifies the contracts: the three-field sub-answer schema + closed enums, the `delivered-now` predicate, the completion-floor drop rule + config knobs, and the sidecar `drop_reason` field (inline AC + session log are not the durable record).
- [ ] The Pass-3 floor consumes the exact enum value `containment=="limited-to-closed"` (no value drift vs the sub-call).

## Acceptance Criteria (sidecar schema + ADR cross-refs)

- [ ] `_slim()` in `sidecar.py` gains a `drop_reason` field (enum {`null`, `"completion"`, `"novelty"`}): `null` for surfaced/normal findings, `"completion"` for completion-floor drops, `"novelty"` for novelty-floor drops; it lives in the per-finding slim payload and is the join key that disambiguates the two floors offline.
- [ ] ADR 0008 (novelty floor) and ADR 0002 (drift-refresh path) are updated with a cross-reference to ADR 0024 so the three Pass-3 floors/paths are documented as one coherent set (no standalone/misleading floor reference).

cross-ref the new third Pass-3 floor (ADR 0020)
